entry.title: while creating a mail work flow error message showing " Enter a mail text" .
entry.content: while creating a mail work flow error message showing " Enter a mail text" even though I have typed the mail text.

entry.title: Re: SOLMAN 7.2 software required for new installation

i know its for sql and a bit old but it will give you a good starting point

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/solution-manager-7-2-installation-and-configuration-i-installations/ba-p/13178062

entry.title: Re: Product Multi Unit Management and POD
entry.content: Hi Vivek Savant, Thanks , it work now.

entry.title: SAP Service and Asset Manager Licensing: What You Need to Know about Mobile Services Entitlements
entry.content:
SAP Service and Asset Manager is a mobile application designed for maintenance and field service execution. Deployed on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Mobile Services, it provides customers with a robust, standard application that includes pre-defined business objects, enterprise asset management content, service management content, and seamless integration capabilities—offering a valuable alternative to building a custom mobile solution from scratch.

The app is deployed on BTP Mobile Services to leverage multiple powerful features, such as the SDK/MDK extension framework, Cloud Connector integration for backend connectivity, and offline functionality. Since BTP Mobile Services is fundamental to the app's architecture, it has been bundled into the licensing model of SAP Service and Asset Manager. Over the years, this licensing model has evolved alongside the product itself. Here’s a look at the progression of material codes descriptions for SAP Service and Asset Manager since its initial release:

  1. SAP Asset Manager for ERP
  2. SAP Asset Manager
  3. SAP Service and Asset Manager
  4. SAP Mobile Execution and Dynamic Forms

If you’re an existing customer, your specific material code depends on the start date of your contract, but each of these codes historically included an equivalent number of BTP Mobile Services users as those licensed for SAP Service and Asset Manager. 

The evolution of SAP Service and Asset Manager’s licensing has coincided with changes in BTP Mobile Services plans. Various service plans have been introduced or deprecated, leading us to the focus of this post:

What Does “Deprecated” Mean for the BTP Mobile Services Standard Plan?

You may have noticed the “standard” plan in your BTP environment labeled as “deprecated.” If you hold an active contract with one of the former material codes, you can continue using the standard plan until your contract renewal date. The deprecation notice won’t impact your technical usage.

If you don’t want to use the deprecated Mobile Services standard plan, consider to replace your existing material code with the latest one, SAP Mobile Execution and Dynamic Forms. This includes usage rights to basic-app-plus plan for internal SAP Applications.

What to Expect When Renewing Your Contract

Upon renewal, your sales representative will update your material code to the latest version, named SAP Mobile Execution and Dynamic Forms. The BTP Mobile Services team has also introduced a new service plan called “basic-app-plus,” specifically tailored for SAP internal applications like SAP Service and Asset Manager. 

Exception for Neo Customers

For customers still operating on a Neo environment, which is scheduled for sunset in 2028, there is an exception. Neo access rights are granted only for the older SKUs. If you’re planning a migration to Cloud Foundry in the coming years, your sales team can work with you to secure approval for continued use of the previous material codes. More details about the Neo phase-out are available in this SAP blog post.

Key Steps for Customers with CPEA+Subscription-Based or PAYG+Subscription-Based Global Accounts

If you have a CPEA agreement with a subscription-based BTP global account, it’s essential to select either the “standard” or “basic-app-plus” plan when using SAP Service and Asset Manager. Otherwise, usage may trigger additional billing for BTP Mobile Services. For instance, if you licensed the app under material code named SAP Service and Asset Manager but opted for the resource plan instead of the standard plan, Mobile Services may bill you as if you were developing a custom app.

Setting Up the Basic-App-Plus Plan for New and Existing Users

For new customers using the latest material code, SAP Mobile Execution and Dynamic Forms, or existing customers switching to this code, follow these steps to ensure you’re leveraging the right entitlements:

  1. Log into your BTP subaccount where your Mobile Services entitlements are provisioned.
  2. (Optional) Verify the source of your entitlements.

 

  1. Go to Entitlements > Edit > Add Service Plans, search for Mobile Services, and select the basic-app-plus service plan for your global account.
  2. Access Instances and Subscriptions in Mobile Services, select an app with license type set to resources or standard, and change it to basic-app-plus (available only if the entitlements are configured correctly).

Step by step video here: https://dam.sap.com/mac/u/a/qQuwTM2?rc=10&doi=SAP1142392 

For new app creation, there’s a streamlined experience in Mobile Services. Create a new app and, when choosing a custom app, specify SAP Service and Asset Manager as an internal SAP application—an option available only with the basic-app-plus plan.

Alternatively, if you were using the app create program from the backend to automatically create your new application in BTP, the prompt is now defaulting the basic-app-plus plan as well. Details here: 3504991 - SAP Service and Asset Manager 2410 Mobile Services App Create Updates

Additional Licensing Updates

With the latest material code (SAP Mobile Execution and Dynamic Forms), customers now receive additional BTP entitlements:

  • 3 users/month for SAP Business Application Studio (BAS)
  • A fixed entitlement of SAP Dynamic Forms (documents/FUE User)

These additions expand on the services included with previous SKUs (3GB of Cloud Foundry runtime and BTP Mobile Services), ensuring that customers have all necessary tools to operate SAP Service and Asset Manager effectively without requiring extra BTP service licenses.

For additional questions feel free to reach out or talk to your sales representative.

SAP Service and Asset Manager SAP Mobile Services SAP Business Technology Platform

entry.title: Hi All,I'm facing a issue in hana xsa project/db build.
entry.content:
Hi All,

I'm facing issue while building a project/dB in hana xsa.

When I take a fresh branch of code from git and clone it in web ide and build it works fine, but when I make a simple change like adding a space or any other minor change the build is failing by throwing error like duplicate view name exists / dependent view exists.

Is anyone aware of such issue?

 

Regards,

Abdul

entry.title: Re: CDS Annotations: How to not display a field on the List Report Column but keep then on the OData
entry.content: To hide a field in the UI but retain it in metadata for business calculations in OData v2 with Fiori Elements, try using @Consumption.hidden: true. This annotation hides the field in the UI but keeps it available in the metadata, allowing it to be used in calculations without displaying it in the List Report. To learn more about effective Fiori Elements and OData practices, visit 5starsstocks .com.

entry.title: 2H 2024 Product Release ILX LRN-156393
entry.content:
In regard to the new ILX Team View Page for Managers

1) Help is unclear what specific manager activities cannot be performed via the new Team View page and need be completed via the legacy experience please advise

https://help.sap.com/docs/PRODUCTS/8e0d540f96474717bbf18df51e54e522/0bf78bb9845b41df9154957c5fd6e0b7.html?locale=en-US&version=cloud

2) Whilst we try to get a handle on these limitations is it possible to turn off the new Team View page and have managers just launch the legacy view?

entry.title: SAP Time Management T510S settings help
entry.content:
Hi experts, I have the following scenario that I would like to solve in T510S WITHOUT using DWS or Day Types.

I would like to solve it (if possible) using T510S Conditions Time.

The scenario is,

Any shift finishing between 8:00 and 18:00 gets no wage type. Example: shift starts 23:00, and finishes 8:30, no shift allowance.

Any shift finishing between 18:00 and 23:59 gets all worked hours at wage type 1ABC (20% shift allowance). Example: shift starts 13:00, finishes 21:30. Wage type 1ABC 8.5hrs

Any shift finishing between 24:00 and 07:59 gets all worked hours at wage type 1ABB (30% shift allowance). Example: shift starts 21:00, finishes 5:30. Wage type 1ABB 8.5hrs

I know I can solve this using DWS Class but I would like to see if this can be solved using T510S Conditions Time as time entries come from clocks.

Thanks......

entry.title: ARIBA GB 2NV integration with S4
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are planning for 2NV integration between SAP ARIBA and SAP S4 systems.
could you please help us on the below query. Thanks!

  1. IN 2NV model, a PR is created in Ariba GB.
  2. system initiates BUDGET CHECK in S4 system and gives back the result to ARIBA.
  3. once the PR is submitted, approval process will be started in ariba.
  4. Once the PR is fully approved in ARIBA, a PO is generated automatically in ARIBA.
  5. Is this PO can be editable in S4.
  6. if the answer to above question no. 5, what could be the next steps.. like the changes will be reflect in ARIBA PO as well? and a new version of the PR will be generated at Ariba

SAP Ariba Buying and Invoicing #Guided buying #SAP ARIBA 2NV INTEGRATION

entry.title: Re: Enable Now Companion content not showing up in browser
entry.content:
Hi lben,
I also had the same problem as you. You can add these parameters in WEB_ASSISTANT_HELP_PLUGIN, Please follow the guide in this link: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ENABLE_NOW/502d46c81ecd4ce29cd29a7c47f1e057/47f09df96dac1014a64bb512c3161826.html

entry.title: Re: Fiori app for work centre in SAP PS.
entry.content:
Hi Anmol

You can use the Fiori App - CR01 for Work Center Creation. Or you can also expose CNR1 on FLP take the help of your Fiori Team to enable it in the FLP. 

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('CR01')/S29PCE 

Best Regards

Sammar

entry.title: Re: Build Your SAP BTP Tech Radar with LeanIX
entry.content:
Great article !

Hope to see if we can get more than 7 rings for various intermediate ring categories in v4 soon.

entry.title: Re: How can I create Credit Memo in Real Estate Management?
entry.content:
Hi Onireke,

There are 2 ways to create a credit in RE-FX:

  1. If you have already made a posting e.g. 1000 p/m and you want to reduce that you can update the amount to e.g. 800 and the system will automatically create a 200 credit. You will get a warning message when you update the condition that is already posted because the system will create a retrospective posting. Also make sure what time period the credit is for because you might have to create a time slice which the system will automatically prompt for in the warning message that you will receive. Whenever you make your change ALWAYS simulate the cashflow first before saving so you can see the impact of your change. If it is not correct just exit without saving and redo your change.
  2. Normally customer will have a condition type for "rent" and a separate for "rent credit" or "abatement" or "discount" depending on what it is called locally but they all do the same. The configuration for the account symbols is configured in reverse than what is done for the rent condition type so that a manual reversal can be entered, without touching the original rent posting.

Hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: How can I create Credit Memo in Real Estate Management?
Is there any way to show a chart in the MDK web platform?
entry.content: I’m currently developing a web application using the Mobile Development Kit (MDK) and need to display charts. However, I’ve encountered an issue where the ChartContent section is not supported in the WebClient. Are there any alternative methods or workarounds to display charts within the MDK web platform? Any guidance or suggestions would be greatly appreciated. Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Copy Cross Model with Adv. Formula to derive Cost Center
entry.content: Hello @N1kh1l , I have a scenario where my Target model have Cost Center and Plant and my Source model has only plant. But my Cost Center has attribute Plant, how can I copy data in this case. My requirement is to copy the Measure value of Plant in Source model to all Cost centers within the plant in Target Model- Regards, Raghu.

entry.title: Re: Export Configuration CBC
entry.content:
Hi Annika,

You can follow SAP Help Portal: Downloading Template Configuration to export.

You can follow below document to upload.

Implementation Activities:

  • Upload configuration content via spreadsheet to maintain the business configuration data.

    Note: This upload function is currently enabled for only a few configuration activities.
     
    Best Regards,
    Stephen

entry.title: Re: How to Pass Dynamic Selection Field in SAP ABAP Submi...
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm trying to execute RFBILA00 with specific profit center (PRCTR) values using WITH FREE SELECTIONS. However, when I run the code, the report executes but includes all profit centers, not just the one(s) I specified.

Here is my code:

DATA : trange TYPE rsds_trange,
      trange_line LIKE LINE OF trange,
      trange_frange_t_line LIKE LINE OF trange_line-frange_t,
      trange_frange_t_selopt_t_line LIKE LINE OF trange_frange_t_line-selopt_t,
      texpr TYPE rsds_texpr.

LOOP AT s_values INTO DATA(lv_prctr_value).
trange_line-tablename = 'FAGLFREESEL'.
trange_frange_t_line-fieldname = 'PRCTR'.
trange_frange_t_selopt_t_line-sign = 'I'.
trange_frange_t_selopt_t_line-option = 'EQ'.
trange_frange_t_selopt_t_line-low = lv_prctr_value.
IF lv_prctr_value IS NOT INITIAL.
APPEND trange_frange_t_selopt_t_line TO trange_frange_t_line-selopt_t.
ENDIF.
ENDLOOP.

APPEND trange_frange_t_line TO trange_line-frange_t.
APPEND trange_line TO trange.

CALL FUNCTION 'FREE_SELECTIONS_RANGE_2_EX'
EXPORTING
field_ranges = trange
IMPORTING
EXPRESSIONS = texpr.

SUBMIT rfbila00
WITH sd_ktopl-low = lv_ktopl
WITH sd_bukrs-low = lv_bukrs
WITH bilavers = '1000'
WITH bilaspra = lv_spras
WITH bilbjahr = lv_report_year
WITH B-MONATE = s_bilabmon
WITH bilvjahr = lv_compare_year
WITH V-MONATE = s_bilaVmon
WITH FREE SELECTIONS texpr
EXPORTING LIST TO MEMORY
AND RETURN.

Issue:

When I debug, I can see that teXpr is populated and passed to the SUBMIT statement, but my specified profit center values are not filtering the report. Instead, the report outputs data for all profit centers.

What I’ve Tried:

  1. I confirmed that the values in trange_frange_t_line are correct before calling FREE_SELECTIONS_RANGE_2_EX.
  2. I checked that the table name (FAGLFREESEL) and field name (PRCTR) align with the data structure in the report.

Any insights on why the FREE SELECTIONS aren’t applying the profit center filter correctly? I would greatly appreciate any suggestions on how to correctly format teXpr or troubleshoot further.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: How to Pass Dynamic Selection Field in SAP ABAP Submi...
entry.content: hi

entry.content:
Hi Sap Experts!

In SAP S/4 Hana Public cloud, for Integrating E-Invoicing and E-Way Bill can we call external API Directly without having any middleware (CPI)?

Help me in how we can integrate E-Invoice and E-Way Bill in SAP Public cloud?

Thanks in advance,

Sai Bhargav

entry.title: Fiori Import sales Order - Custome Excel Template
entry.content:
Hi All,

 

We currently have the Import Sales Order (App ID F4293) application up and running in our production system. This is working fine by standard functionality, but our business users have requested an improvement. 

In Import Sales Order, there is a template that is downloaded and used by users to input their sales order data before being imported in the application. 

Certain Columns that are required need to be filled manually (since business copy pastes data from  a reference) 

*Sales Order (Temporary ID) and Item (Temporary ID) 

I have created a formula which auto fills these fields for the user.

Now the question is if there is a way to update the excel that is being downloaded from the fiori app and replace it with an updated one I have amended which includes this formula.

 

I have already reached out to our development team regarding this, but they are still familiarizing themselves with this process. Any helpful insight is much appreciated (where to look, transaction codes to visit)

entry.title: Re: New or not to the SAP Community, share your story!
entry.content: Hi...I am RajKishore. I am Happy to join the SAP community, I have about 17 Years of Experience in SAP. Worked in the technical areas using ABAP, Fiori Elements, CDS Views, AMDP, RAP, ODATA, Event Based Architecture, Workflows etc. Looks forward to connect with the community, learn and share knowledge. Thanks.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content: Hi SAP Community, I am Rajkishore from MN, USA.

entry.title: Re: Framework Order in SAP S4hana Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear Kaeratiya,

In S/4HANA Public cloud there is no object Framework Order. This functionality is covered by an additional tab in the Standard PO. Please explored the Manage Purchase Order with Purchase order limits functionality.

Regards

Sahil Grover

__PRESENT

entry.title: Re: Unified Joule Setup: Bridging Simplicity and Performance
entry.content:
@nageshcaparthy "Thank you for sharing this insightful article on the unified Joule setup! I found the detailed overview on bridging simplicity with performance in Joule's framework particularly valuable. The approach outlined really showcases SAP's commitment to making complex functionalities more accessible and streamlined for users.

I'm curious about potential future enhancements: Are there plans to further integrate Joule with other third-party tools for even greater flexibility? Additionally, could we expect to see any AI-driven analytics or predictive capabilities embedded directly within Joule to proactively support decision-making?

entry.title: Re: Profit center in Public Cloud
entry.content: Hi, Profit centers are organizational units that reflect a management-oriented view of the organization for the purposes of internal control. They allow you to analyze areas of responsibility and delegate responsibility to decentralized units, treating such units as "companies within the company." Profit center is the foundation for divisional accounting. I wondered why would you want to turn it off. If you could share what you expected and not expecting, we might could find other ways to achieve that. 'https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f0d042517a434458b171ff5867b18445/6baea0aebb1d4d54a1156f2698382c94.html best regards, liling

entry.title: Re: Profit center in Public Cloud
entry.content: Hi, Profit centers are organizational units that reflect a management-oriented view of the organization for the purposes of internal control. They allow you to analyze areas of responsibility and delegate responsibility to decentralized units, treating such units as "companies within the company." Profit center is the foundation for divisional accounting. I wondered why would you want to turn it off. If you could share what you expected and not expecting, we might could find other ways to achieve that. 'https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f0d042517a434458b171ff5867b18445/6baea0aebb1d4d54a1156f2698382c94.html best regards, liling

entry.title: Re: Configuring reciever port for outbound IDOC
entry.content: Check BD84. And after that check LS S17EWM100 (asume it´s a testsystem?) you can find the port in detail section of Outbound parameters for SHPMNT

entry.title: Re: Purchase Order creation via Excel upload
entry.content:
Hi Isoma,

You can use Migration cockpit, SAP Build Automation  or create you application using PO API.

https://help.sap.com/docs/IRPA_S4HANA/6cd2b6d425154d2188eb8256628bced0/736003a6e7644f67858ad97537269e9e.html

Regards

Sahil Grover

__PRESENT

entry.title: Re: How to provide default date with cap actions
entry.content:
Hi,

Default values for action parameters are possible - please see the fe V4 specific section "Default Values for Action Parameters" in page.

Best Regards,

   Sandeep

entry.title: HOW IS SPECIAL STOCK INDICATOR DETERMINED IN PP rework order?
entry.content:
Hi PP Guru,

I'd like to know how the special stock indicator 2 (project) is determined when it comes to creating Rework order by CO07.

1. There is NO stock determination rule for the order type in OPJ2.

2. When specifying WBS in the initial screen of CO07, the special stock indicator 2 (project) is auto determined, which is what we want. 

3. When no WBS is specified in the initial screen of CO07 and instead maintain WBS in the assignment tab, the special stock indicator 2 is always blank.

If manually specified with 2, we got the error message CO684.

So what is the difference and impact of specifying WBS in the initial screen and header?

Please kindly help and query.

Thank you,

Sally

entry.title: Re: HANA Model Management Menu not Visible
entry.content: We have found that it seems to only work if you are a super user and of course in the CCC only operators and PSU accounts are superusers but I have not been able to do enough testing to prove this conclusively but some partners encountered this scenario.

entry.title: Re: Sending Email notifications for Time Valuation Alerts
entry.content:
Hi @NeeleshKamath ,

Thanks for the wonderful blog. It is really helpful.

I have a question, As per your blog, when user enters something today then alert would be sent out(as you have given condition time valuation date today()) in on save rule, but we have time recording admissibility of two weeks so how to send out mail when someone entered something in past weeks.

 

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: how to change password of b1 cloud domain administrator on windows
entry.content: There is an SAP Note that discusses this in detail - https://me.sap.com/notes/2649948

entry.title: Re: Can we integrate external API of Cleartax for EInvoic...
entry.content:
Dear Sai,

We have SAP standard solution for same. Please use a certified GSP

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/634261119fec4d58970471f2c4a9a740/b9524d0554714ab6b0dacc8866c0fac9.html 

If you plan to use Non-certified GSP then customize the standard iflows as per your requirement.

https://api.sap.com/package/SAPeInvoiceIndia/integrationflow

https://api.sap.com/package/SAPeWayBillIndia/integrationflow

CI is recommended but yes you can use API calls as you described. But for this you have to build your own application entirely.

Regards

Sahil Grover

entry.title: The holy trinity of Integration
entry.content:
Imagine you have a good old diesel car. It somehow let's you travel from A to B. But it is missing air conditioning, the radio only memorizes 5 stations and the most digital feature is the quartz clock on the dashboard. When arriving, you will be pretty tired, have listened to unwanted country songs and have hit multiple traffic jams. But that is still far better than a horse carriage.

On your way to your way to work it suddenly breaks down. Fixing it would cost more than a new car.

What do you do?

Do you buy another 80s station wagon?
or...
Do you go to the dealership-street in your area and look for a future-proof, fast and easy to use car?

Well - believe it or not - some people seem to favor the first option - also when it comes to an Integration Platform: they would rather continue doing text-file transfer, run scheduled jobs and install local agents everywhere.

Fortunately some customers decide to embrace the future and redesign the communication of their systems from the ground up. This is one of these stories.

 

The context

Let's take the example of the company Gebr. Heinemann.

They are operating as a retailer and wholesaler with a global and end-to-end supply chain, and need to get integration right to serve their customers in the best way.

Gebr. Heinemann is represented at airports and border crossings, on cruise ships and ferries, in planes and downtown shops, as well as in diplomatic missions and special military zones.

All these locations require different payment options (stationary, mobile, per app...) and specific services (product service, travel service, sales service, ...) and will connect to many different backends (SAP, Visma, ...).

 

Having all the systems smoothly interact with each other means that there is a need for different communication patterns, that can be combined as needed:

  • data massage: data is sent from one system to another and the payload is transformed (converted, enriched, filtered, mapped, ...).
  • asynchronous communication: the data is sent out in a "fire and forget" mode, allowing for strong decoupling and real-time communication.
  • synchronous communication: the calling system expects a response to his query in order to continue processing.

Instead of using their former "SAP Process Orchestration" to connect all these applications, they turned towards SAP BTP Integration Suite and SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh. These SAP BTP services, as well as the Kyma runtime, are the cornerstones of the GH Checkout Platform.

In the next chapters, we will use the example of the price journey to explain the implementation in detail.

 

Prices and promotional offerings ("Data Massage")

The product prices are stored in an SAP Customer Activity Repository (CAR) system, located on-premises at GH.

These prices are influenced by promotional pricing like bulk discount ("3 for the price of 2"), discounts due to coupons or discounts for card holders. The complete configuration is stored in SAP Omnichannel Promotion Pricing Service (OPPS).

You already guessed it: prices need to be synchronized from SAP CAR to SAP OPPS. This needs data massage and processing, and has been implemented in SAP Integration Suite, Cloud Integration.

Indeed, Cloud Integration provides connectivity, data transformation and orchestration capabilities. It will:

  • extract the price data from SAP CAR (or any other backend system),
  • perform data transformation (like filtering) using predefined flow steps,
  • load the data into SAP OPPS.

Also, using SAP Integration Suite, Cloud Integration, allows GH to stick to their architectural principles: on-premises systems should always be connected through a middleware. This eventually decouples the various backend systems from their shops, simplifying overall operations.

 

Price distribution to the shops ("Asynchronous communication")

Once the sales prices are available in SAP OPPS, they need to be distributed to both cash registers and electronic shelf labels, physically located in the shops.

Distributing (initial load and updates) more than 300.000 product prices means updating more than 600.000 electronic shelf labels (ESLs) and cash registers on a regular basis. The ESLs and cash registers are managed by specific providers, so GH doesn't actually take care of the last mile communication.

In order to cope with this magnitude of updates, Gebr. Heinemann has chosen to use SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh (AEM) which receives price data in dedicated queues and topics, and makes them available to any consumer, like the ESL or cash register SaaS.

Because OPPS is not yet event-enabled (on the roadmap), a microservice has been implemented that generates price changing events, based on deltas in SAP OPPS. These events are then published to AEM. Note that this microservice runs in an SAP BTP Kyma runtime environment.

When publishing the price changes, the topics may look something like this:

price/update/eu/mondelez/chocolate/toblerone/white

Consumers can subscribe to all specific price update events using this dynamic subscription:

price/update/eu/*/chocolate/>

Eventually, both ESL and Cash Register infrastructures listen to these events through the Solace Message Format (SMF) protocol, which offers very comprehensive features like dead-letter queue, dynamic topics, rejection of events, optimized latency, ....

The managed services of the cash registers and ESLs then distribute the new prices to the hardware, without GH having to intervene. 

 

The payment ("Synchronous communication")

Once a customer has decided to buy the special offer, he makes his way to the cash register.

During the payment process, the cash register requests the final price based on promotional offers in real-time from OPPS.

Indeed, a customer could use one or more promotions (like a coupon, his loyalty card and/or a "multibuy offer".) But OPPS makes sure that only the right price is paid by the customer and that impossible combination are not used. This cannot be done by the cash register or the ESL, hence OPPS provides that final price - in real-time - during the payment.

In order to simplify and safeguard the access to the OPPS backend system, SAP Integration Suite, API Management (APIM) comes into play. It sits between the cash register and OPPS and enforces central governance for both runtime and design-time:

  • Runtime: Security, monitoring, internal monetisation,
  • Design-time: documentation, versioning and standardization of APIs.

This central API governance helps GH to run efficiently by enforcing standards centrally and enforce re-use or APIs.

 

 

Summary

At the end of the day, GH has implemented an agile, real-time and resilient Checkout Platform based on SAP Business Technology Platform based on communication patterns specific to hybrid landscapes.

 

Through the pricing and payment example we dove into the details of the platform. However, there are many more use cases implemented on it, and many more are yet to come. But they all share one thing in common: thanks to the standardization, modular approach and re-usable patterns, requirements can be implemented in a cost-effective and fast way.

Last but not least, SAP Process Integration has no relevance anymore to Gebr. Heinemann: the old platform has been replaced by a modern and future-proof IPaaS providing all necessary communication patterns. Obviously, introducing these new concepts came at the cost of organizational changes, education and new mindset but the result was definitely worth the journey.

 

Special thanks

A big thank you goes out to Benedikt Althaus and Stefan Franke who prepared and delivered an awesome Integration Roundtable presentation in September 2024, and who spent a lot of time discussing the content of the blog with me!

entry.title: Re: Wokzone
entry.content: Hi Abdul, Thanks for the quick response, The issue is due to the SAML from the basis side and once they removed we can able to access the applications in both inside VDI and ousiide VDI

entry.title: Kanban stock Transfer Strategy using Warehouse request. (Different Kanban Status Sequence)
entry.content:
Hello There,

We are using newly released solution from SAP Embedded EWM on Kanban (A Stock Transfer with Warehouse Request.) With this all fine but we are looking forward to use different Kanban Status during process.

Ref.

https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blogs-by-sap/sap-s-4-hana-for-manufacturing-logistics-2310-support-of-kanban-with/ba-p/13578896

Currently when Kanban status is EMPTY meaning warehouse request is created. We want to use IN PROGRESS and IN TRANSIT as well if possible.

Please help with how to map this process in SAP EWM from WR (empty)-->Warehouse Task (in Progress) --??

 

Thank you in Advance,

Abhinay Waikar

entry.title: S/4HANA 構築手順に関する質問
entry.content:
【質問】
現在、社内向けにS/4 HANAのオンプレミス環境（TDD版）を構築したいと考えておりますが、構築の手順が分からずに困っています。
サービスライセンス公布書類にManage My Servicesにて、ライセンスキーを作成しソフトウェアをダウンロードしてくださいと書いてあったのですが、現在のアクティベイトしているサービス（SAP Test and Demo User, starter package）に対応しているライセンスキーとソフトウェアが分かりません。
また、ライセンスキーの使用方法もわかっていないので教えていただけますと幸いです。
何卒よろしくお願いいたします。

【現状】
SAP Test and Demo User, starter package（TD_7016852）を契約しており、
TDD版S/4HANAをオンプレ環境で構築しようとしています。
OSはSUSE Linux Enterprise Server15 SP 6を使用しておりソフトウェアをインストールできる環境は整っています。

また、ライセンスキーやソフトウェアに関しては手探りで作業をしており、
ライセンスキーとして
Product of Installation Number : SAP S/4 HANA
Add New Systemとして
　Product SAP S/4 HANA
　Version SAP S/4 HANA, on-premise edition 1511
を作成しようとしています。

また、ソフトウェアとしては
SAP S/4 HANA / SAP S/4 HANA 2023/
SAP HANA PLATFORM EDITION 2.0 (INSTALLATIONS AND UPGRADES)
をインストールしようとしています。

ソフトウェアのインストールに関してはSAP Download Managerからダウンロードできることを確認済みです。
大変恐縮ですが、どなたかご回答をいただけますと大変助かります。

entry.title: Re: Shared row axis in two tables in SAP Analytics Cloud...
entry.content: Share row axis is not supported in SAC. But for your requirement, you can simply create a restricted measure( restrict to period with navigation to year to date) and display with periodic measure side by side.

Yes, 
SAP Test Automation Tool is only available for S/4Hana public Cloud.

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud for planning - Is it truly a limi...

entry.title: Re: AFO SAC Edition and Standard Edition installed simult...
entry.content: You don't have to.  AfO standard edition covers all the features AfO SAC edition provides you.

entry.title: Re: Configuring Multiple Communication Configurations wit...
entry.content:
Dear Katja,

The way would be to use router in CI to call the necessary iflow.

The router in SAP Cloud Integration (CI) is a filter that routes messages to the right destinations based on specific conditions. The router can be used to: Speed up integration, Control message flows, and Improve error handling.

Communication arrangement is called based on the business partner assigned to the communication arrangement and not on BRF+.

So if document 1 has business partner A, the communication arrangement its linked to will be called

if Document 2 has business partner B, then communication arrangement its linked to will be called.

Regards

Sahil Grover

__PRESENT

entry.title: SAW 17: Question on JSON parsing (dbo.sp_parse_json procedure)
entry.content:
Hi,

SAP SQL AnyWhere 17.0.10.

There is some JSON packet. I pass it to the procedure "sp_parse_json" for processing

declare cJSON long varchar;
call dbo.sp_parse_json('sql_array', cJSON);

I will get the parsed JSON package into the variable "sql_array".

Question: How do I check "sql_array" for the presence of a certain field in a JSON packet? JSON-package, whether this field is there or not ?

entry.title: CSRF Token Missing Error in Android OData Service
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I’m currently facing an issue with my application. It works perfectly in Cloud Foundry (Web), but when I deploy it in Mobile Services (Android), I encounter a “CSRF token is missing” error when posting data.

I’m working on an Android application that interacts with an OData service, and I need guidance on retrieving the CSRF token. I understand that CSRF protection is crucial for secure API calls, but I’m unsure how to implement this in my Android app.

Could anyone provide a step-by-step approach or share code snippets on how to obtain the CSRF token for an OData service? I’ve checked my configurations and ensured that CSRF protection is enabled, but the problem persists.

Has anyone else experienced this issue? Any insights or solutions would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you in advance for your help!

I also do not know how to use the license key, so I would appreciate it if you could tell me how to use it.

Current status
I have a contract with SAP Test and Demo User, starter package (TD_7016852),
I am trying to build TDD version of S/4HANA on-prem environment.
I am using SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP 6 as the operating system and have the environment to install the software.

I am also trying things out about the license key and software,
The license key is
Product of Installation Number : SAP S/4 HANA
Add New System
Product SAP S/4 HANA
Version SAP S/4 HANA, on-premise edition 1511
as a license key.

In addition, as the software
SAP S/4 HANA / SAP S/4 HANA 2023/
SAP HANA PLATFORM EDITION 2.0 (INSTALLATIONS AND UPGRADES)
I am trying to install the software.

I have already confirmed that I can download the software from the SAP Download Manager regarding the software installation.
I would greatly appreciate it if someone could give me an answer to my question.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) - JDBC Adapter - Step-by-step guide - Pre-requisites and Configurati
entry.content:
Hi @ChingHong , 

I am using the same approach to insert more 100k record with per batch 10k records, but the problem is with the Non-Atomic Mode, even though I am using the Non-Atomic mode, for large value constraints failure it is falling whole operation. i.e. even in 10K record if one record is failed due to the length constraints it is unable to insert all 10k record. though it is perfectly working for the duplicate constraints case i.e. in 10k Records if multiple records failed due to the duplicate entry error, successful record it is able to insert. So my question is does Non_atomic only work for the duplicate entry error ?

  • How to categorize software collections?
  • What is the best and correct practice for assigning key user extensibilities to different kind of software collections?

entry.title: SAP Solution Manager のデータが最新であることを確認方法
entry.content:
2.SAP Solution Manager のフォローアップ活動の実行

下記項目の実施内容が分からず相談した次第です。
[項目]
・SAP Solution Manager のデータが最新であることを確認します
[手順]
・システムデータのチェック
[実施内容]
システムランドスケープディレクトリ (SLD) で、システムランドスケープが最新です。これを行うには、最新のソフトウェアコンポーネントかどうかを確認します。

上記実施にあたり、参考になるNote等あればご教示いただくことは可能でしょうか。

以上よろしくお願いいたします。


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP Solution Manager のデータが最新であることを確認方法
entry.content:
ご担当者　様

平素よりお世話になっております。

お問い合わせ頂いたSAP Solution Manager のデータが最新であることを確認するには、SAP Solution Manager／LMDBにて登録済の管理対象システムの情報が最新かどうかをご確認頂きたいことという認識が合っていましょうか。

それでは、下記のステップに従ってSolution Manager LMDBで管理対象システムの詳細をご覧下さい。

トランザクションLMDB -> 管理対象システムSID（Solution ManagerのSIDで入力してもOK） -> システム概要  -> Detail(詳細) -> データサプライヤー情報で下記の情報をご確認できます。

  • 最終手動変更記録 
  • データサプライヤーによる最終変更 
  • 作成日時 
  • データサプライヤー一覧

ご不明な点等ございましたらお知らせください。

以上、宜しくお願いいたします。

SAPサポート

entry.title: Re: error in cl_sxml_string_writer, sXML Library
entry.content:
Due to the discussion here, our development has introduced the following enhancements with releases 9.14/2502, 8.16/2025:

Header Options for sXML Library

Header options for the XML declaration are now available in the sXML Library:

  • CO_OPT_VAL_NO (no XML header), this is the default option.
  • CO_OPT_VAL_WITHOUT_ENCODING (version)
  • CO_OPT_VAL_WITHOUT_ENCODING (version and encoding)

entry.title: SAP Goods consumption document posting date should be same as POD confirmation
entry.content:
Hi friends,

We are implementing the POD process and have identified an issue during the process.

POD Date does not match voucher posting date

Could you tell me what caused this?

Is there a good solution?

Await your kind feedback

Thanks

entry.title: setup windows cluster for SAP HA
entry.content:
I have problem when setting Windows cluster before installation SAP Netware 7.52. please help.

Could you please share cluster requirements/reparation for installing sap HA?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: setup windows cluster for SAP HA
entry.content:
Setting up a High Availability (HA) cluster for SAP systems involves careful planning and adherence to specific requirements. Below are the general steps and requirements for preparing a cluster environment for SAP HA:

1. Hardware Requirements

  • Servers: At least two servers are needed for a basic HA setup to ensure redundancy.
  • Storage: Shared storage (like SAN) is required to store the SAP database and central services. The storage must support clustering.
  • Network: Redundant network interfaces and proper network infrastructure to support communication between cluster nodes and client systems.

2. Software Requirements

  • Operating System: Ensure a supported operating system is used (here is Windows Server).
  • Cluster Software: Depending on the OS:
    • Windows: Windows Failover Clustering.
  • Database: Supported database that can be clustered (e.g.,  Oracle, SQL Server).
  • SAP Software: Ensure you have the correct SAP applications that support HA.

3. Network Configuration

  • Configure a dedicated network for cluster communication (heartbeat).
  • Set up additional network interfaces for public/client communication.
  • Use static IP addresses for reliability.

4. Shared Storage Configuration

  • Set up and configure shared storage with LUNs (Logical Unit Numbers) as required by SAP and the database.
  • Ensure proper LUN masking/zoning is configured so that each server has access to the shared storage.

5. Cluster Software Installation and Configuration

  • Install the cluster software on each node.
  • Configure cluster resources such as virtual IP addresses, cluster services, and any application-specific agents (e.g., SAP-specific cluster agents).

6. SAP Installation and Configuration

  • Install SAP in a way that supports HA. This typically involves installing central services in cluster mode.
  • Configure SAP to use the shared storage for central services and the database.

7. Database Setup

  • Install and configure the database in HA mode, ensuring it uses shared storage for database files.
  • Set up database replication or log shipping as required by the specific HA setup.

8. Testing and Validation

  • Perform failover tests to ensure that the SAP system can switch over to a secondary node without significant downtime.
  • Validate that data integrity is maintained during failover.

Best Regards,

entry.title: Re: MB52
entry.content: All the materials are S priced still anount is not getting posted

entry.title: SAP HANA Cloud Network Security: Best Practices for IP Allowlisting and Access Control
entry.content:
When managing SAP HANA Cloud, network security is a critical responsibility for administrators. To keep your data and applications secure, SAP HANA Cloud offers flexible IP allowlisting options that let you control which IP addresses can access your instance. Currently, HANA Cloud supports three configurations for network access:

  1. Allow access only from Business Technology Platform (BTP) — the default setting.
  2. Allow access from all IP addresses — open access.
  3. Allow access only from specific, trusted IP addresses — customized access.

 

Why IP Allowlisting Matters

In a secure HANA Cloud environment, controlling who can connect to your instance is essential. Allowlisting IP addresses adds a layer of protection by limiting network access to only those addresses you deem trusted. This is especially important in production environments, where even minor security oversights can result in unintended exposure or unauthorized access.

For HANA Cloud admins, it’s recommended to use the most restrictive allowlisting option possible for their needs. Let’s explore the IP allowlisting options available and when to use each one.

1. Allow Only BTP IP Addresses

This option restricts access to IP addresses associated with Cloud Foundry within the same BTP region. When selected, only applications and tools within the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Cloud Foundry environment in the same region as your HANA Cloud instance can connect.

  • Use Case: This option is ideal for instances that exclusively interact with BTP applications or tools. For example, a HANA instance in the EU10 region will only accept connections from Cloud Foundry IPs in EU10.

  • Limitation: Tools or applications located in different regions (e.g., a tool in US10 trying to connect to EU10) will not be able to access the instance. This restriction ensures a high degree of security by preventing external access from other BTP regions.

  • Recommendation: This is the default setting and is suited for applications tightly bound to a specific regional environment within BTP.

2. Allow All IP Addresses

This option opens access to the HANA Cloud instance from any IP address, effectively allowing worldwide connectivity.

  • Use Case: Use this setting only for instances requiring global access, such as development or testing environments where multiple teams across regions may need connectivity.

  • Limitation: This is the least secure option, as it does not restrict access based on IP addresses, which can expose your instance to unwanted traffic or attacks.

  • Recommendation: It’s generally recommended to avoid this option in production environments due to the high security risk. Only use it if absolutely necessary for unrestricted access, such as in short-term or non-critical environments.

3. Allow Specific IP Addresses and IP Ranges

This option allows you to specify trusted IP addresses or IP ranges (using CIDR notation) that can access your HANA Cloud instance. With this setup, only applications or users from these allowed addresses, as well as the default Cloud Foundry IPs in your region, can connect.

  • Use Case: This option is highly recommended especially for production environments where you want to tightly control access, ensuring only known IPs can connect. For example, you might specify the IP range 192.168.1.0/24 to permit only addresses within this private range.

  • Flexibility: You can define multiple IP ranges to accommodate dynamic or changing IPs within a subnet. If Jenkins nodes or other services have dynamic IPs, consider using ranges like 172.17.0.0/24 to cover IPs that might vary across this subnet.

IP ranges are defined by CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) notation, which uses a suffix (like /24, /32, etc.) to determine how many IP addresses are included in a range. Here’s a brief overview of some common options and where you can learn more.

Common CIDR Notations and Their Ranges

  • /32: Single IP address (e.g., 192.168.1.1/32 includes only 192.168.1.1).
  • /31: Two IP addresses (typically used for point-to-point links).
  • /30: Four IP addresses (often used for very small networks).
  • /29: Eight IP addresses, e.g., 192.168.1.0 to 192.168.1.7.
  • /28: Sixteen IP addresses, e.g., 192.168.1.0 to 192.168.1.15.
  • /27: Thirty-two IP addresses, e.g., 192.168.1.0 to 192.168.1.31.
  • /26: Sixty-four IP addresses, e.g., 192.168.1.0 to 192.168.1.63.
  • /25: 128 IP addresses, e.g., 192.168.1.0 to 192.168.1.127.
  • /24: 256 IP addresses, covering a whole "C class" subnet, e.g., 192.168.1.0 to 192.168.1.255.
  • /16: 65,536 IP addresses (a full "B class" network), e.g., 192.168.0.0 to 192.168.255.255.

Where to Learn More

  1. Online CIDR Calculators:

    • Websites like CIDR.xyz or IP Calculator let you input any CIDR notation and show the full range of IPs it includes.

  2. Documentation and Tutorials:

    • Cisco's Guide to Subnetting provides thorough explanations of CIDR and subnetting: Cisco Subnetting Guide.
    • AWS Documentation on CIDR is also a great source, especially if you’re working with cloud environments: Amazon VPC Documentation on CIDR.

  • Additional Configuration for Multi-Region Access: If you need to connect from different BTP regions, be sure to add the necessary NAT IP addresses for those regions to your allowlist.

  • Recommendation: This is the best practice for production environments, as it provides the highest level of control over network access.

Summary of Recommendations for HANA Cloud Admins

SAP HANA Cloud provides flexible and secure network options for controlling instance access:

  1. Use "Allow only BTP IP addresses" for single-region setups where only Cloud Foundry applications need access.
  2. Avoid "Allow all IP addresses" in production due to its open access policy, which can expose your instance to risk.
  3. "Allow specific IP addresses and IP ranges" is recommended for production environments, where security is essential. Use CIDR notation (like 192.168.1.0/24) to define trusted IP ranges efficiently.

By implementing strict IP allowlisting, HANA Cloud admins can secure their environments while maintaining flexibility in development, testing, and production scenarios. In any setup, remember that IP allowlisting alone is one part of your security toolkit—continue to monitor and manage access as part of a comprehensive security strategy.

 

Additional Reference : 

sap-hana-cloud-network-and-security 

hana-cloud-domains-and-ip-ranges 

 

Thank you for reading this blog post!  If you have found this blog post beneficial in your learning journey, please consider giving it a ‘‘Like’ ,‘Share’ it with your friends and followers, and ‘Follow’ me for more content like this. Your support means a lot to me!
If you encounter any problems, have feedback, or need help, feel free to leave a comment or contact the SAP Community for assistance.
https://www.linkedin.com/in/showkath/

entry.title: Re: 🇮🇳 SAP CodeJam ABAP: SAP BTP, ABAP Environment & RAP
entry.content:
Hi @djorason ,

This event was closed long back. If you are interested to host SAP CodeJam do look at the SAP CodeJam page and let us know. we can take the discussion forward.

SAP CodeJam: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam-blog-posts/so-you-want-to-host-a-codejam-everything-you-need-to-know/ba-p/221415

entry.title: How to call SAP ByDesign API from S4HANA Cloud System
entry.content:
I would like to call Sales Order API(ByD) from S4hana Sales Order BAdI logic.

Target is that when I save SO in S4 system, SO data from S4 will pass to ByD via API.

Is that possible to do? Is there any reference for this requirement?

Best Regards,

Khaing Zin

entry.title: Re: 4 Steps to getting SAP SuccessFactors Best Practices Content in your Customer Implementations
entry.content: Why it is mentioned "We recommend that this switch is unchecked once your implementation activities are over. Another recommendation is not to activate this switch in the Production environment." Could you please provide more details.

entry.title: SAP ERROR MESSAGE M3332
entry.content:
Hi, I can't create a material because of this message. How can i solve it? My alternative UoM is not the same as base UoM.

Diagnosis

You have entered an alternative unit of measure that is the same as the base unit of measure. These units must be different.

Procedure

Only enter an alternative unit of measure if it differs from the base unit of measure. Otherwise, leave this field blank.

entry.title: Re: Triggering workflows for MDF using OData API
entry.content:
Hello Team,

Can I also pass on the workflow comments? and HOW??

entry.title: Re: 4.3 Web Intelligence Data Lineage Challenge
entry.content: Hi,

entry.title: Re: com.sap.cds.services.impl.ContextualizedServiceException: No ON handler completed the processing
entry.content: Hi.. Were you able to resolve this issue? I'm facing the same error. I would really appreciate some help. Thanks

entry.title: SAP BW: Bex Query Input Help - Error 9999 has occurred in the BWASAP HANA server
entry.content:
Hello team,

Some users got error message when trying to select Input Help, but Error 9999 has occurred in the BWASAP HANA server.

Could you help deep check the issue? Thanks a lot.

Detail step could be found in the attachment.

entry.title: Re: SAP Stammtisch Luzern
entry.content:
Hallo @DavidPortmann 

Auf jeden Fall 😀 Mittlerweile stehen auch die Themen unseres Stammtisch Events fest:

  • Wiederverwendbares UI5 Control
  • Microservice auf der BTP 

Schön, dass du dabei sein wirst.

LG und bis bald, Marco

entry.title: Re: On what makes a great frontend framework and why UI5 is one
entry.content: Hi @frankmeertens, thanks for the feedback. And I agree!
